dsl_rule.translation_notes: Signal describes breakout trading from 2-standard-deviation range. Mapped to Bollinger Bands (which use 2-std-dev by default) with price_pos_20 to identify position relative to 20-day mean. Entry triggers when price breaks outside bands. Confirmation requirement and hourly monitoring cannot be enforced at rule level but should be configured in backtest engine parameters. Exit at opposite boundary (bb_lower for long, bb_upper for short) or 1% stop loss. Direction marked CONTEXT_DEPENDENT as signal applies to both long and short breakouts.

backtest_rule.reason: Signal describes breakout trading from 2-standard-deviation range. Mapped to Bollinger Bands (which use 2-std-dev by default) with price_pos_20 to identify position relative to 20-day mean. Entry triggers when price breaks outside bands. Confirmation requirement and hourly monitoring cannot be enforced at rule level but should be configured in backtest engine parameters. Exit at opposite boundary (bb_lower for long, bb_upper for short) or 1% stop loss. Direction marked CONTEXT_DEPENDENT as signal applies to both long and short breakouts.

source_rule_text: A trader should establish predetermined price levels (breakout thresholds) for monitored instruments and maintain alert systems to trigger immediate action when those levels are breached, regardless of time of day or market session.
